These are standard and do not process untrusted data internally; any risk would depend on how these methods are used externally, which is outside the scope of this code snippet.\n- **Scores consistency:** All malware scores are 0, which is appropriate given the content. The risk scores are very low, reflecting the non-executable, interface-only nature of the code.\n\n### Final assessment:\n\n- **Malware score:** 0 — no signs of malicious behavior.\n- **Obfuscated score:** 0 — code is clear and autogenerated.\n- **Risk score:** 0–0.1 — negligible, primarily because of the connection method which could be misused if misused externally, but not inherently risky.\n\n**Overall conclusion:**  \nThis code is a standard, autogenerated interface factory for a smart contract. It contains no malicious, obfuscated, or suspicious elements.
